page_content:
WGSS212 Women in Western Europe 1750-Present (3 Credits) An analysis of the economic, family, and political roles of European women from 1750 to the present. The effects of industrialization on women's work and status, the demographic parameters of women's lives, and women's participation in political events from market riots to suffrage struggles. Cross-listed with: HIST212. Credit Only Granted for: HIST212, WMST212 or WGSS212. Formerly: WMST212.

WGSS230 Introduction to Humanities, Health, and Medicine (3 Credits) An overview of the historical, cultural, ethical, and spiritual dimensions of medicine, human health, disease, and death from the points of view of various humanistic disciplines. Cross-listed with: ARHU230, ENGL254, HIST219N. Restriction: Permission of ARHU-English Department. Credit Only Granted for: ARHU230 , ENGL289C, ENGL254, ARHU298A, HIST219N, or WGSS230.
